inline_comments.message:
I know this was not done in this change, but returning null is dangerous. An Option<Integer> should be returned, or Optional<Integer> depending on if VAVR is available in this asdctool or not.

Another (better) approach could be to return a Try<Integer>. The successful branch would return a Try.of(status) while the error branches would respectfully return a Try.failure(ProductDeletionException(message)) and a Try.failure(ProductDeletionException(message, exception)).

This would ensure that no matter what happens, a non-null value is always returned. Making functions total is actually a best practice.

Some references:
- https://www.baeldung.com/vavr-try
- https://www.javadoc.io/doc/io.vavr/vavr/0.9.2/io/vavr/control/Try.html

I agree, i have opened another pr to change those stuffs.
Thanks
